title: Wire frontend to Motoko backend for student registration and document upload

requirements.summary: Update frontend React Query hooks to wire registerStudent and uploadDocument backend calls, ensuring Profile page student creation and Documents page file upload persist data through the ICP backend actor
acceptanceCriteria: useRegisterStudent mutation calls the backend registerStudent actor method
useUploadDocument mutation calls the backend uploadDocument actor method with student_id, document_name, and file data
Profile page successfully creates a student record on form submit
Documents page successfully records an uploaded document linked to the current student
React Query cache is invalidated after successful mutations so lists refresh automatically

file_operations.description: Update useRegisterStudent mutation to call actor.registerStudent with all six fields (fullName, email, mobile, course, category, income) from the backend interface. Ensure the mutation returns the newly created student ID and invalidates the 'myStudent' query key on success. Update useUploadDocument mutation to call actor.uploadDocument with studentId, documentName, and filePath (base64 or filename). Ensure it invalidates the 'documents' query key on success. Verify both mutations handle errors gracefully and surface backend authorization traps to the UI.

Wire the Profile form submit handler to call the useRegisterStudent mutation when creating a new student record. Map form fields (fullName, email, mobileNumber, courseName, category, annualFamilyIncome) to the registerStudent parameters. On success, store the returned student ID and refetch the student profile. Ensure the form validates all six required fields before submission and displays success/error toast messages.

Wire the file upload handler to call the useUploadDocument mutation with the current student's studentId, the document name/type, and the file content (convert to base64 Text if needed). On success, invalidate the documents query to refresh the list. Ensure the upload button is disabled when no student record exists and display appropriate error messages for authorization failures.
